summary: ecto-nightly-spirit postinstall exfiltrates env, filesystem secrets, and HTB flags to hardcoded remote IP
details: On `npm install`, postinstall.js executes a credential- and flag-harvesting routine on Linux hosts whose cwd matches sandbox paths (/tmp/pkg, /home/scan, /home/node). The script enumerates the entire process.env, reads /flag.txt and other candidate files, and shells out to `find` and `grep -RaoE 'HTB\{[^}]+\}'` across /root, /home, /etc, /var, /opt, /tmp, /workspace to collect secret-shaped files and HTB flag strings. It also gathers uid, hostname, ps output, and other host diagnostics. The collected data is PUT as JSON to a hardcoded bare-IP endpoint at http://154.57.164.70:30569/api/modules/ECT-472839 (with 127.0.0.1/localhost tried first as fallback). In addition, the script writes attacker-controlled content into web-server document roots (/usr/share/nginx/html/flag.txt, /var/www/html/flag.txt, /app/public/flag.txt, /srv/http/flag.txt), creating directories as needed. The combination of install-time env/filesystem credential harvest + outbound exfiltration to an attacker-controlled IP + planting files into system web roots is unambiguous offensive behavior.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
